Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search Linear for issue number 219 (or title "Launch Readiness Checklist") to check its status, assignee, and documented blockers.
- Search Slack for channel history or threads discussing ticket 219 or "Launch Readiness Checklist" to understand the reasons for delay.
- Create a comment on Linear ticket 219 (via linear_create_comment or similar) asking the assignee to update if no clear blocker is documented in the ticket or Slack.
- Search Linear for issue number 220 (or title "Stipend duplicate detection") to check its current state (e.g., todo, in_progress).
- Search the Airtable Stipend Transactions table (via airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblStipends00001) to retrieve expense records for Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics, and cross-reference against the 47 auto-processed pilot expenses from the audit (ENG-216) to confirm all 47 are accounted for.
- Create a comment on Linear ticket 220 (via linear_create_comment or similar) documenting any missing expenses found, or confirming everything matches.
- Search Contacts for Dmitri Volkov (via contacts_search_contacts) to retrieve his email address (dmitri.volkov@moveops.com) for the calendar invite.
- Check the state of ticket 220. If it has not been started (state is not in_progress), create a calendar meeting with Dmitri Volkov (via calendar_add_calendar_event) with the specified description: "If you have already started with implementation you can provide updates on Slack offline, otherwise this meeting will be used to discuss blockers in implementation."
- Post a summary message to the #engineering channel (via conversations_add_message) covering the status of both tickets and the relaunch readiness assessment.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
- Search Linear for issue number 219 (or title "Launch Readiness Checklist") to check its status, assignee, and documented blockers.
- Search Slack for channel history or threads discussing ticket 219 or "Launch Readiness Checklist" to understand the reasons for delay.
- Create a comment on Linear ticket 219 (via linear_create_comment or similar) asking the assignee to update if no clear blocker is documented in the ticket or Slack.
- Search Linear for issue number 220 (or title "Stipend duplicate detection") to check its current state (e.g., todo, in_progress).
- Search the Airtable Stipend Transactions table (via airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblStipends00001) to retrieve expense records for Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics, and cross-reference against the 47 auto-processed pilot expenses from the audit (ENG-216) to confirm all 47 are accounted for.
- Create a comment on Linear ticket 220 (via linear_create_comment or similar) documenting any missing expenses found, or confirming everything matches.
- Search Contacts for Dmitri Volkov (via contacts_search_contacts) to retrieve his email address (dmitri.volkov@moveops.com) for the calendar invite.
- Check the state of ticket 220. If it has not been started (state is not in_progress), create a calendar meeting with Dmitri Volkov (via calendar_add_calendar_event) with the specified description: "If you have already started with implementation you can provide updates on Slack offline, otherwise this meeting will be used to discuss blockers in implementation."
- Post a summary message to the #engineering channel (via conversations_add_message) covering the status of both tickets and the relaunch readiness assessment.

outcome

outcome

The comment was successfully created.

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The ENG-220 comment explicitly states 'The ticket has been in Todo since March 15 despite your comment indicating Layer 1 would start that following Monday,' and the Slack message states 'the ticket status has not changed.' The 'todo'/not-started status is cited as the reason for the follow-up meeting.
✓Run #2The ENG-220 comment states 'This ticket is still in `Todo` state with no evidence of implementation having started' as the basis for requesting implementation status, and the calendar invite was created because the ticket had not been started. The Slack message also notes 'State: Todo' as the reason for the follow-up.
✓Run #3The ENG-220 comment states 'The ticket is still in Todo and the last update was March 15,' and the calendar description states 'This ticket has been in Todo since March 15 with a due date of March 25,' explicitly tying the meeting scheduling to the ticket's Todo/not-started status.
✓Run #4The comment on ENG-220 explicitly states: 'Additionally: This ticket is still in `todo` state (due 3/25, now 4+ weeks past due).' The calendar description also references the ticket being 'in todo state since 3/15.' The 'todo' status is clearly cited as the context for the meeting.
✓Run #5The Slack message states 'the ticket remains in "Todo" state' in connection with scheduling the Dmitri meeting. The ENG-220 comment and the Slack summary both reference the Todo/not-started status as context for the follow-up meeting.
✓Run #6The ENG-220 comment states 'This ticket is still in `Todo` state. Your 3/15 comment and Slack message indicated you'd begin Layer 1 on the following Monday, but no state change has occurred in 6 weeks.' The meeting was explicitly scheduled because the ticket remained in Todo/not-started state.
12 The agent's analysis of the missing expenses in the tracking file correctly restricted the scope to the Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics pilot data, without including records from unrelated clients like BrightLoop.
outcome
 ✓✓✗✓✗✗ 50%
✓Run #1The ENG-220 comment lists only Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics pilot expenses. Despite the Airtable data containing BrightLoop Analytics records (Jordan Ekwueme), the agent correctly excluded them from the audit analysis, restricting scope to the two pilot clients.
✓Run #2The ENG-220 comment lists only Vectral Systems (6 of 28 expenses tracked) and Mosaic Robotics (4 of 19 expenses tracked), with no mention of BrightLoop or other non-pilot clients. The analysis was correctly scoped to the pilot data only.
✗Run #3The ENG-220 comment incorrectly includes two BrightLoop records (items 11 and 12: 'Jordan Ekwueme — BrightLoop — Monitor Stand — $45' and 'Jordan Ekwueme — BrightLoop — WiFi System — $299.99') in the list of pilot expenses. BrightLoop is an unrelated client; the pilot data should have been restricted to Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics.
✓Run #4The ENG-220 comment correctly references only Vectral and Mosaic Robotics pilot data ('23 correct Vectral approvals + 16 correct Mosaic approvals') without mentioning unrelated clients like BrightLoop. The scope was correctly restricted to pilot data.
✗Run #5The agent included BrightLoop Analytics records (Jordan Ekwueme's monitor stand and WiFi entries) in the ENG-220 comment's list of 12 present Airtable records. The criterion specifies the analysis should be restricted to Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics pilot data, excluding clients like BrightLoop.
✗Run #6The agent included BrightLoop Analytics records in its analysis (Jordan Ekwueme's monitor stand and WiFi records), even though the audit scope was restricted to the Vectral and Mosaic pilot data. The agent's response also mentions '~18 unique pilot-era records are in Airtable (across Vectral, Mosaic, and BrightLoop)', incorrectly including BrightLoop in the scope.
13 ```text The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., linear_get_issue, linear_list_issues, or linear_list_comments) to check the status of ticket ENG-220 and/or look up Dmitri Volkov's updates. ```
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #5The agent called linear_list_issues with query 'ENG-220' and also called linear_list_comments with issueId 'issue_stipend_duplicate_detection' to check the ticket status and Dmitri's updates before deciding to schedule the meeting.

14 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records) to access the stipend transaction tracking data for verifying the 47 pilot expenses.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called `airtable_list_records` with `base_id: 'appMoveOpsOps001'` and `table_name: 'Stipend Transactions'` to retrieve the stipend tracking data, which was then used to cross-reference against the 47 pilot expenses from the audit.
✓Run #2The agent used airtable_list_records with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name 'Stipend Transactions' to access the stipend transaction tracking data for verifying the 47 pilot expenses.
✓Run #3The agent called `airtable_list_records` with `base_id: 'appMoveOpsOps001'` and `table_name: 'Stipend Transactions'` to retrieve the stipend tracking data for verifying the 47 pilot expenses.
✓Run #4The agent called `airtable_list_records` with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name 'Stipend Transactions' to access the tracking data for verifying the 47 pilot expenses. It also called `airtable_list_tables` and `airtable_list_bases` to discover the correct base/table.
✓Run #5The agent called airtable_list_records on the 'Stipend Transactions' table and multiple airtable_search_records calls filtering by Account (Vectral Systems, Mosaic Robotics, BrightLoop Analytics) to verify the 47 pilot expenses.
✓Run #6The agent called airtable_list_records and airtable_search_records on the 'Stipend Transactions' table in appMoveOpsOps001, retrieving pilot expense tracking data to verify against the 47-expense audit scope.
